entries.garrytan/gbrain: This project implements a self-wiring knowledge graph that combines vector retrieval with automated entity extraction for highly structured memory. It is a top-tier choice for power users, exposing over 30 MCP tools to integrate structured memory directly into Claude Code and Cursor workflows.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight focuses on long-term agent learning through biomimetic data structures that facilitate retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It allows Hermes agents to build evolving mental models and facts over time, supporting integration across major providers like OpenAI and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses a multi-agent loop—including roles like coach and architect—to accumulate validated knowledge across repeated runs. It is designed to help agents succeed on complex investigations by utilizing verifier-driven checkpoints and browser-integrated research.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides a lossless context engine that uses hierarchical DAG summaries and immutable SQLite storage to ensure no message data is ever deleted. It offers a three-level escalation system for context compaction, allowing agents to grep and expand history without losing critical details.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This self-hosted backend enables private, cross-session memory for Hermes agents through a robust stack of Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis. It is the ideal solution for developers requiring full control over their memory infrastructure while maintaining compatibility with any OpenAI-style LLM provider.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that uses a unified SQLite vault to persist memory across different agents without cloud dependencies. It features a hybrid retrieval system combining BM25, vector search, and graph traversal to automate decision capture and handoffs.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format, making agent memories human-readable and portable. It leverages zero-cost local retrieval through hybrid embedding search to automate memory injection for Hermes and other MCP-compatible tools.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases and media with automatic OCR and summarization. It organizes information using bidirectional edge tags, creating a navigable web of relationships accessible via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system optimized for sub-millisecond retrieval using a native SQLite-vec and FTS5 hybrid search. Its unique three-tier BEAM architecture separates working, episodic, and scratchpad memory for high-performance local agent operations.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict and cache relevant information. Built as an MCP-native server, it utilizes local GGUF models and SQLite-vec to provide a low-latency, 'future-aware' RAG experience.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a three-tier word replacement system. It ensures Hermes-generated text maintains a human-like quality through a rigorous two-pass refinement process.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular cross-platform library that equips agents with frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills specification within the Pydantic AI framework. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate six types of professional diagrams, including UML and ML architectures, from natural language descriptions. It includes a five-round self-check safety valve to ensure coordinate precision and iterative refinement.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interoperability, this skill enables Hermes to interact with Chainlink Data Feeds and execute cross-chain transfers via CCIP. It provides a standardized way for agents to monitor smart contracts and manage local testing workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming tool allows Hermes to weave runnable code and prose together into synchronized markdown documents. It features a 'tangler' to extract source files and automatically updates documentation when code edits are detected.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: This plugin enables cross-instance shared memory, allowing Hermes to remember past work and extract decision-quality data for model fine-tuning. It creates an end-to-end pipeline for an agent to document its own logic and train its eventual replacement.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: A comprehensive registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with built-in monetization via Stripe and blockchain. It automates dependency resolution and provides a CLI for local installation of OpenAPI-driven skills.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: This official integration provides standardized prompting guidelines and API patterns for the FLUX image generation model family. It allows Hermes to handle precise hex color specifications and structured JSON prompting for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities through observation.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: This harness manages long-running missions through structured planning, milestones, and local-first state snapshots. It facilitates seamless handoffs between agent runs by generating detailed markdown briefs and validation assertions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill provides Hermes with a framework for autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks. It features real-time progress tracking and built-in rollback procedures to ensure safe recovery from execution errors.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: A progressive MCP client that utilizes a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize context window usage. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for dynamic configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This utility converts existing repositories and developer documentation into agentskills.io-compliant instruction sets. It maps AI instructions directly to specific source code lines, ensuring high-precision skill generation from legacy codebases.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community-driven hub for browsing and installing Hermes skills with integrated security scanning for prompt injection. It supports micropayment protocols for premium content and features an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scores.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem using the C# MCP SDK. It enables automatic skill discovery and utilizes progressive disclosure to maintain efficient token usage in C#-based agent environments.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This collection provides daily-updated Nix packages for a wide range of AI coding tools, including Hermes and Claude Code. It is ideal for users who need a unified proxy API and multi-agent orchestrators within a reproducible Nix-based environment.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: This security-focused deployment uses NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking to provide hardware-enforced sandboxing for Hermes. It includes over 40 integrated tools and pre-configured security policies that range from strict inference to permissive system access.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services with a focus on reproducibility. It features secure secrets handling via sops-nix and supports a hybrid skill architecture for both managed and interactive installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It integrates local model management, GPU monitoring, and a workflow engine for scheduled automation directly on the desktop.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker-based sandbox that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this headless deployment configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, allowing the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs and integrate with Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates with Portainer to deploy Hermes alongside a ttyd web terminal for browser-based interaction. It simplifies the deployment lifecycle by using GitHub Actions and GHCR for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is ideal for Hermes deployments requiring strict oversight, featuring the Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off and integrated spend monitoring.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides an MCP-native architecture for building autonomous swarms that support 23 model providers, including Hermes. It distinguishes itself through runtime skill learning and an interactive organizational chart that visualizes agent delegation and team hierarchies in real-time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to refine harness code for Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, compete for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents across both teams, visualizing their tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a real-time dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates 17 specialized agents across the entire development lifecycle, from initial research to infrastructure deployment. It ensures high-fidelity outputs by implementing mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing across multiple LLM providers.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native SDLC by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. The system executes an autonomous eight-phase workflow, enforcing architectural integrity through automated graph-diff gates at every stage of development.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: Focusing on isolated delegation, this tool routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It prioritizes execution safety through configurable timeouts and strict output character limits for all delegated workflows.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI tracks token consumption and real-time costs across over 20 AI coding agents, including Hermes and Claude Code. It utilizes LiteLLM pricing data to provide developers with detailed 3D visualizations and usage analytics for their local and cloud-based model interactions.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows for deep visual customization of the Hermes terminal interface through YAML-based themes and ASCII art presets. Developers can create partial skins that inherit default values or swap between community themes like Skynet and DOS to personalize their agent's workspace.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for native execution without external processes or OAuth requirements. It specifically enhances the environment with MLOps capabilities for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent supervision, exposing structured page schemas and metadata via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It provides a human-visible interface for auditing agent actions, featuring persistent sessions and annotated checkpoints to maintain state during complex web tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework introduces per-folder scoping for Hermes, allowing developers to define project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions. It enables seamless switching between the default global agent and specialized, scoped agents tailored to specific directories and local configurations.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A high-speed static linter that identifies structural bugs in agent configurations and system prompts without requiring LLM calls or API keys. It uses seven specialized detectors to provide automated quality gating and HERM v1.1 scoring for agentic CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of agent personas, memories, skills, and environment credentials to ensure workspace continuity.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script provides a turnkey Docker stack for Hermes Agent, integrating LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a zero-cost local setup. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social features, optimized for routing to free models and local GPU inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-build web interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI through a streamlined three-panel layout for chat, file browsing, and session management. It is designed for high performance on both desktop and mobile browsers using a lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript architecture.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard that centralizes multi-platform configuration for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp alongside real-time token and cost analytics. It features a versatile multi-backend file browser that supports local, Docker, SSH, and Singularity environments.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This security-hardened workspace offers a native web experience featuring real-time SSE streaming for chat and tool call rendering. It includes a dedicated memory browser and skill manager for agent orchestration, protected by execution approval prompts and authentication middleware.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: Acting as a multi-agent gateway, this self-hosted dashboard allows users to start, stop, and configure multiple agent profiles from a single interface. It provides detailed token analytics and a browser-based terminal to monitor system metrics and agent status in real-time.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS workspace that facilitates direct SSH connections to Hermes agents without requiring remote helper services. It features a multi-tabbed terminal with live theme controls and profile-aware management for sessions, skills, and cron jobs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: This mobile-first React SPA brings a terminal-style chat experience to the Telegram Mini App ecosystem, supporting streaming responses and slash commands. It allows for background agent spawning with live output monitoring and integrated system health gauges.
entries.Web3CZ/Web3Hermes: This localized Chinese interface provides a tailored experience for regional users with dark and light theme support and comprehensive session export features. It includes an integrated workspace file browser for direct editing and visualization of tool calls.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app designed to simplify the initial setup of Hermes Agent through guided installation and dependency resolution. It supports a wide range of LLM providers and local endpoints while managing messaging gateways for platforms like Discord.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: Focused on process monitoring, this dashboard provides real-time activity tracking and cost distribution analysis for Hermes sessions. It features FTS5 search capabilities for inspecting conversation history and a read-only interface for managing scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes tool execution timelines and SSE streaming for a transparent agent experience. It features an integrated marketplace for skills, MCP servers, and plugins, alongside isolated profile management for separate memory and keys.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic web interface uses a unique single-file React architecture that requires no npm dependencies for deployment. It includes a real-time artifact panel for live HTML, SVG, and PDF previews, alongside management tools for MCP extensions and memory files.
